item.title: NYT Strands #818 Hints and Answers for Today, May 30, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Strands is a daily word search puzzle game from The New York Times. It is a relatively new game, launched in 2024, in which players seek a common theme among words in a 6×8 letter grid.

Whether it’s your first time playing or you’re a Strands veteran, today’s tips and strategies can help you find words faster. So, if you’ve found yourself stuck with today’s Strands, don’t worry; below you’ll find some helpful hints and even the answers for Strands Game #818 for Saturday, May 30, 2026

To play today’s Strands, head to the game’s website.

IMPORTANT: The following information includes spoilers on today’s edition of Strands. If you don’t want the answer given to you, stop scrolling.

How To Play Strands

Strands is a daily word search that challenges you to find words with a common theme. There’s also a Spanagram that reveals the deeper theme behind the words.

  • Words that are three letters or fewer do not count.
  • Words that reveal the correct theme appear in blue.
  • The Spanagram turns yellow when found.
  • Finding three words that don’t fit the theme will reveal a hint to help you find the proper words.

The goal is to string together as many words as possible. Every letter on the grid gets used! Daily puzzles keep players coming back for more, making it a fun brain exercise and a popular online trend.

Strands can only be played on the New York Times website or on the NYT Games app.

Strands Hints for Today, May 30, 2026

The Strands’ answers to the theme of Game #818 are all terms related to good qualities to have in friendship.

General Strands Hints for May 30

Without giving today’s Strands answers completely away, here are a few helpful hints for today to get you started:

  • Hint 1: Word to describe someone who always has your back.
  • Hint 2: When you’re trusted with many tasks at your job, you likely have this quality.
  • Hint 3: Deserving of your faith and belief.

Use these clues to narrow down your searches and increase your chances of solving Strands #818 for May 30, 2026.

Words to Help You Find Strand Hints for May 30, 2026

If you want help getting started without having today’s Strands answer given to you, there are a few starter words worth trying that will help you unlock some in-game hints. A few starter words that may help are:

  • GOOD
  • BAIL
  • BAILER
  • WORTH
  • WORDS

Today’s Strands Answer for Game #818 on May 30, 2026

If you’ve tried everything, including using the above hints, and are still stuck, you can find the answers to today’s Strands below. Check out the answers for past puzzles here.

  • LOYAL
  • HELPFUL
  • RELIABLE
  • TRUSTWORTHY
  • CARING
  • SPANAGRAM: GOODFRIENDS

If you solved today’s Strands without our answers, congratulations! If not, take note of the best tips above for tomorrow’s puzzle, and check back daily for more hints.

When Is The Next Strands Game After #818?

All New York Times games are updated daily at 12 am or Midnight in your respective time zone. Information about the next game is often available in the hours leading up to it, giving players a heads-up. The game on May 31 will be Strands Game #819.

What Is Strands?

Strands is a daily word search puzzle game created by The New York Times. Launched in 2024, Strands challenges players to discover a common theme among words in a 6×8 letter grid. The game’s original concept was developed by Juliette Seive, a research director on The New York Times Games team, with the aim of replicating Wordle’s success. Tracy Bennett has served as the puzzle editor for Strands, overseeing the creation and curation of daily puzzles since the publication’s launch.

For even more Insider Gaming delivered straight to your inbox, sign up for our weekly newsletter.

MOBILE APP

Level Up Your Gaming News

Real-time news, exclusive podcasts, push alerts, and a better reading experience.

Available on iOS & Android

item.title: 007 First Light Reached 1.5 Million Sales in 24 Hours
item.content:encoded:
007 First Light achieved 1.5 million sales in just one day, giving IO Interactive a strong start to its James Bond franchise. In the process, the developer broke its own record for critical responses.

As of this writing, the game sits at an 87 on Metacritic, the highest rating for any IO Interactive title. First Light is also the best-rated Bond game in 30 years.

IO Interactive Celebrates 1.5 Million Sales for 007 First Light

In a press release, IO Interactive touted the game’s success. In its statement, the team boasted that the way players have embraced the title proves the success of the vision it laid out to honor the Bond character’s legacy. Of course, 007 was created by Ian Fleming and has consistently survived, thrived, adapted, and pioneered across several decades and forms of media.

“The milestone of 1.5 million units sold in 24 hours reflects overwhelming global enthusiasm for 007 First Light and represents a significant achievement for IO Interactive. With an 88 OpenCritic rating and 87 Metacritic score, 007 First Light has been embraced by both critics and players alike, already establishing itself as one of 2026’s most successful gaming releases. The sales recorded across all platforms underscore the game’s broad appeal and the successful execution of IO Interactive’s vision to honor Bond’s legacy in gaming,” the statement reads.

For more on 007 First Light, check out a comprehensive list of every celebrity cameo in the new title. The game is available now on PC, Sony PlayStation 5, and Xbox Series X|S. Furthermore, a Nintendo Switch 2 version is slated to release later this year.

Sound off about this story and all others on our official Discord server. Also, don’t forget to sign up for our weekly newsletter.

MOBILE APP

Level Up Your Gaming News

Real-time news, exclusive podcasts, push alerts, and a better reading experience.

item.title: Former Sega VP Says He’s Heard a Sonic CD Remake Is in the Works
item.content:encoded:
Mike Fischer is the former VP of entertainment marketing at Sega of America. He worked for the company during its rise and heyday between 1990 and 1997. He would also have a brief stint in the company between 2001 and 2003.

He has credits on games such as Sonic Adventure DX, Crazy Taxi 3, and Jet Set Radio Future. Now, he’s speaking about the potential remake of a Sonic title on one of the peripherals of the Sega Genesis. It’s a game he calls his favorite in the franchise.

Sonic CD Could Be Returning, According to Former Sega Executive

During an interview with Sega-16, Mike Fischer noted that he’s heard rumblings that Sonic CD is being remade. He also noted that he believes Sonic Mania, a modern take on 16-bit Sonic, was the best game in the franchise that Sega has made in years.

“I’ve heard that they’re remaking Sonic CD, which would be wonderful. That was my favorite Sonic game,” he said. “They’re going to have to innovate and create new IPs. It’s interesting to think about that the most successful game they’ve had in recent generations are in the Yakuza series, which was definitely not a made-for-consumer experience. It wasn’t part of that old legacy. The trailer for the new Yakuza game goes across like all of the 20th century.

“It’s really amazing. It’s almost like the Godfather series. I think it starts out like a pre-World War II, like in the 1900s, and then they tell like the story of the game stuck in, and then the next chapter is like post-World War II or something. I look at it, I think, “Wow, they are they are taking the Yakuza series and innovating it in such an incredible way that I cannot wait to play it.” So, if that is a symbol of the new direction that they’re headed, then I’m pretty excited about it. So, they can continue to work with this wonderful classic IP that they have, but they can’t rely on it.”

Sega has actually put a lot of energy into revitalizing classic franchises. It’s known that games like Golden Axe and Streets of Rage are getting new projects outside the video game medium.

Speaking of, it is possible that the remake of Sonic CD Fischer is referring to is the upcoming Sonic the Hedgehog 4 film, which is based on the plot of Sonic CD and introduces Metal Sonic and Amy Rose to the franchise. Learn more here.

Learn more about Sega’s plans to do more with their classic properties at this link. Insider Gaming will continue to monitor the story and report any updates as more information becomes available.

Sound off about this story and all others on our official Discord server. Also, don’t forget to sign up for our weekly newsletter.

MOBILE APP

item.title: XBOX Store Lists (Then Takes Down) Rayman Origins Remaster for $2,000
item.content:encoded:
An Enhanced Edition of Rayman Origins briefly hit the XBOX Store with an obnoxious placeholder price point.

The game originally released back in November of 2011 for PC, the Nintendo Wii, the Sony PlayStation 3, the PlayStation Vita, and the Nintendo 3DS. This new version was listed with a $2,000 tag before the game’s page was taken down.

Rayman Origins Enhanced Edition Features 4K and 60 FPS

The game featured up to four-player couch co-op, over 60 levels, and 100 characters. Of course, the hook for this version is the enhanced visuals.

“Enjoy Rayman Origins like never before with 4K resolution, 60 FPS, and modern enhancements, including new quality-of-life features. Discover 60 hidden Relics across the adventure and track your progress in the Snoring Tree,” read the game’s description.

It is not known when the game will re-emerge in digital shops or what the actual price will be. There’s also no set release date for the upcoming Ubisoft remaster. Insider Gaming will continue to monitor the story and report any updates as more information becomes available.

For more Rayman news, it was recently confirmed that the remake of Rayman Legends is now dubbed “Rayman Legends Retold.” A price point for that game was revealed. Check out all that information, along with details about multiplayer, the physical edition, and more, at this link.

How do you feel about the Rayman franchise? Sound off about this story and all others on our official Discord server. Also, don’t forget to sign up for our weekly newsletter.

MOBILE APP
